text.title: Feature Discovery Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Feature Discovery application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Collect feature ideas from ALL four source types before scoring: Customer Research (interviews, tickets, churn, NPS detractors), Usage Analytics (adoption, drop-off, errors), Competitive Analysis (rival features, negative reviews), Market Trends (industry reports, regulatory shifts). Single-source candidate lists are rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-source discovery creates blind spots; analytics show what, interviews show why, competitors show expectations.

rule.statement: The candidate list MUST be capped at 20 items per discovery cycle. Lists with >20 items are rejected and routed back to collection refinement.
rule.rationale: A list longer than 20 means the collection phase wasn't selective; scoring becomes a distraction not a decision tool.

rule.statement: Every candidate MUST carry a Kano type: Must-have | Performance | Delight | Indifferent. Must-haves block Delighters in the build queue.
rule.rationale: Delighters built on shaky Must-haves fail to retain customers; users who cannot log in are not impressed by AI features.

rule.statement: Effort estimates MUST come from a named engineering lead (XS/S/M/L/XL). Agent-generated effort estimates are rejected as RICE inputs.
rule.rationale: RICE divides by Effort; an LLM-guessed denominator silently mis-prioritises the entire backlog.

rule.statement: Every RICE score MUST record Reach (users/quarter), Impact (0.25/0.5/1/2/3), Confidence (50%/80%/100%) and Effort (person-weeks) explicitly. "Confidence inferred from vibe" is rejected.
rule.rationale: Confidence is the honesty multiplier; opaque confidence inflates scores for features without evidence.

rule.statement: The top 3 RICE-ranked candidates MUST pass a pre-build validation step (fake-door, prototype, Wizard-of-Oz, limited beta, or pre-sales) before engineering commitment.
rule.rationale: RICE scores are estimates; pre-build validation replaces estimates with behavioural evidence before sunk-cost.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
